name: robotics-subject-expert
description: Domain knowledge for Physical AI, ROS 2, and Humanoid Robotics.
capabilities: Provide ROS 2 code examples in Python and C++
Guide robot simulation setup in Isaac Sim and Gazebo
Describe inverse kinematics, SLAM, and reinforcement learning for robotics
author: AbdulSamad94
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only static knowledge about robotics concepts. All 20 static findings are false positives: the analyzer misidentified documentation text (URLs, educational content, markdown formatting) as security threats. No executable code, file access, network calls, or command execution capabilities exist.
valueStatement: Robotics projects require specialized knowledge of ROS 2, simulation environments, and embodied AI systems. This skill provides structured domain expertise for building and troubleshooting physical AI systems.
limitations: Does not execute or modify your robotics code
Cannot connect to live robot hardware or simulators
Cannot install or configure software on your system
Knowledge is based on training data, not real-time robot state

useCases.title: Learn ROS 2 fundamentals
useCases.description: Get structured explanations of ROS 2 concepts with hands-on lab exercises and code examples.

useCases.title: Build physical AI agents
useCases.description: Understand how to integrate AI perception with robot actuation for embodied intelligence systems.

useCases.title: Debug simulation issues
useCases.description: Troubleshoot problems in robot simulation environments and understand sim-to-real transfer challenges.

promptTemplates.title: ROS 2 basics
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how ROS 2 nodes communicate using the DDS middleware. Show a simple publisher-subscriber example in Python.

promptTemplates.title: Robot kinematics
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain inverse kinematics for a 6-DOF robotic arm. How do I calculate joint angles to reach a specific position?

promptTemplates.title: SLAM implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare different SLAM approaches for indoor mobile robots. What are the trade-offs between graph-based and filter-based methods?

promptTemplates.title: Sim-to-real transfer
promptTemplates.prompt: What strategies help bridge the sim-to-real gap when training robot policies with reinforcement learning? Include domain randomization techniques.

bestPractices: Start with ROS 2 fundamentals before tackling complex robot control systems
Use simulation environments like Gazebo to test before deploying to physical hardware
Combine multiple sensing modalities (vision, LIDAR) for robust robot perception
antiPatterns: Ignoring ROS 2 quality of service settings when working with unreliable networks
Skipping simulation testing and deploying untuned policies directly to robots
Overlooking the sim-to-real gap when training reinforcement learning policies

faq.question: What ROS 2 version does this skill cover?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on ROS 2 Humble Hawksbill, the current LTS release.

faq.question: Can this skill write code for my specific robot hardware?
faq.answer: It provides general code examples and guidance. Specific hardware integration requires your implementation.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to my robot simulator?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides knowledge only. You must run simulators independently.

faq.question: Is my robotics code or data accessed by this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill contains no code execution capabilities and cannot access your files.

faq.question: Why is my robot not moving as expected in simulation?
faq.answer: Common issues include incorrect joint configurations, missing dependencies, or timing problems.

faq.question: How does this skill compare to general coding assistants for robotics?
faq.answer: This skill provides deep domain expertise specifically for robotics, while general assistants have broader but shallower knowledge.
